title: EpistemicTrustModel
description: Defines how an agent evaluates the credibility of information sources and scales its belief updates accordingly.

baseline_trust.description: Default trust scores (0.0 to 1.0) for different categories of information sources.

entity_trust_ledger.description: Dynamic ledger of trust scores for specific agents or endpoints.

trust_dynamics.description: Rules for how trust continuously evolves based on validation and observation.

betrayal_penalty.description: Score reduction when source is proven wrong.

validation_reward.description: Score increase when source is proven right.

time_decay_rate.description: Rate at which trust reverts to baseline over time.

observation_feedback_loop.description: Mechanisms for retroactively updating trust based on observing an agent's actions and environment changes.

action_alignment_check.description: If true, reduces trust if an agent's observed actions contradict the information they provided.

consensus_shift_impact.description: How much a source's trust score drops if the rest of the trusted network reaches a consensus that contradicts them.

assimilation_policy.description: Mathematical approach used to combine new info with existing beliefs, weighted by source trust.
